title: linaria vulgaris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Linaria vulgaris subsp. vulgaris, also known as the common toadflax, is a flowering plant species native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides in its leaves and flowers. The medicinal properties of Linaria vulgaris subsp. vulgaris have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, respiratory problems, and menstrual disorders, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
